title: Geoeconomic Monitor: Dire Straits, Again
gist: After three days of US-Iran military strikes in the Persian Gulf, technical talks are set to resume, but a power vacuum atop the Iranian regime makes lasting peace unlikely, pushing oil importers to rebuild petroleum reserves while Trump escalates tariff pressure on trade partners.

themes.theme: Iran-US Persian Gulf conflict and oil security

insights.claim: Iran and the United States traded military strikes in the Persian Gulf over three days, after which technical talks between the two sides are scheduled to resume.
insights.speaker: Gavekal Research
insights.so_what: Watch for whether technical talks produce a durable de-escalation or merely a pause before renewed strikes, given the underlying regime instability.
insights.quote: Following three days in which Iran and the United States traded military strikes in the Persian Gulf, technical talks between the two sides are scheduled to resume.

insights.claim: A lasting Iran-US peace is unlikely because a power vacuum at the top of the Iranian regime is exacerbating internal divisions that undermine the regime's ability to negotiate durably.
insights.speaker: Gavekal Research
insights.so_what: Investors should discount headline de-escalation news and price persistent Gulf tail risk into energy and shipping exposure as long as Iranian leadership remains fractured.
insights.quote: material progress towards a lasting peace will be impossible so long as a power vacuum at the top exacerbates divisions within the Iranian regime

insights.claim: Tom Holland argues that ongoing uncertainty from the Iran-US conflict makes rebuilding strategic petroleum reserves a strategic imperative for oil-importing countries.
insights.speaker: Gavekal Research
insights.so_what: Expect increased strategic reserve buying by oil-importing nations, which could add a persistent bid under crude prices independent of near-term demand signals.
insights.quote: The resulting uncertainty means rebuilding petroleum reserves is a strategic imperative for oil importers, writes Tom Holland.

themes.theme: Renewed US tariff pressure on trade partners

insights.claim: Tom Miller analyzes a renewed attempt by Trump to squeeze US trade partners in the latest round of the tariff war.
insights.speaker: Gavekal Research
insights.so_what: A fresh tariff escalation alongside Gulf conflict risk compounds macro uncertainty for exporters and could pressure currencies and equities of targeted trade partners.
insights.quote: Tom Miller analyzes Trump’s renewed attempt to squeeze US trade partners in the latest round of the tariff war.
